questions.stem: This schematic depicts the organization of the extracellular matrix. The thin, specialized meshwork of type IV collagen and laminin that lies directly between the epithelial layer and the underlying interstitial connective tissue serves principally which role?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Provides tensile strength and elastic recoil to tendons and ligaments

choices.label: B
choices.text: Conducts action potentials between adjacent epithelial cells

choices.label: C
choices.text: Stores oxygen and slowly releases it to hypoxic tissue

choices.label: D
choices.text: Generates contractile force that pulls wound edges together

choices.label: E
choices.text: Acts as a specialized scaffold that anchors and supports epithelial, endothelial, and smooth muscle cells

explanation.summary: The layer shown between epithelium and interstitium is the basement membrane, a specialized extracellular matrix rich in type IV collagen and laminin that provides an anchoring, supporting surface for the growth of epithelial, endothelial, and smooth muscle cells.
explanation.whyCorrect: The layer shown between epithelium and interstitium is the basement membrane, a specialized extracellular matrix rich in type IV collagen and laminin that provides an anchoring, supporting surface for the growth of epithelial, endothelial, and smooth muscle cells.

questions.stem: These stacked cross-sections illustrate repair of a large, gaping skin wound over successive time points. What mechanism is primarily responsible for closing the defect shown?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Complete regeneration of the epidermis without any scar

choices.label: B
choices.text: Surgical apposition of the wound edges with minimal scarring

choices.label: C
choices.text: Filling by granulation tissue followed by myofibroblast-mediated wound contraction

choices.label: D
choices.text: Repair by regeneration of permanent cardiac muscle

choices.label: E
choices.text: Resolution of the wound without any inflammatory phase

explanation.summary: Healing by secondary intention occurs with extensive tissue loss: the large defect fills with granulation tissue, and myofibroblasts form a contractile network that pulls the wound together, reducing the defect over weeks.
explanation.whyCorrect: Healing by secondary intention occurs with extensive tissue loss: the large defect fills with granulation tissue, and myofibroblasts form a contractile network that pulls the wound together, reducing the defect over weeks.

questions.stem: In the clinical image on the right, the raised scar extends beyond the boundaries of the original wound, and the accompanying histology shows thick, brightly eosinophilic collagen fibers arranged haphazardly. What is the most likely diagnosis?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Wound dehiscence

choices.label: B
choices.text: Hypertrophic scar

choices.label: C
choices.text: Keloid scar

choices.label: D
choices.text: Wound contracture

choices.label: E
choices.text: Granulation tissue

explanation.summary: A keloid is exuberant scarring that extends beyond the boundaries of the original wound and shows thick, brightly eosinophilic collagen arranged haphazardly; unlike hypertrophic scars, which stay within the original wound and may regress, keloids do not regress on their own.
explanation.whyCorrect: A keloid is exuberant scarring that extends beyond the boundaries of the original wound and shows thick, brightly eosinophilic collagen arranged haphazardly; unlike hypertrophic scars, which stay within the original wound and may regress, keloids do not regress on their own.

questions.stem: The lower histologic panels of this chronic-wound montage show necrotic surface debris, inflammation, and underlying granulation tissue. Which histologic feature best defines the lesion as an ulcer?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Neoplastic proliferation of squamous epithelial cells

choices.label: B
choices.text: An encapsulated collection of pus with intact overlying epithelium

choices.label: C
choices.text: Full-thickness regeneration of the epidermis with an intact surface

choices.label: D
choices.text: Loss of the overlying epithelial surface with underlying inflammation and granulation tissue

choices.label: E
choices.text: Hyperplasia of keratinocytes forming a raised nodule

explanation.summary: An ulcer is a wound lacking an intact epithelial surface; histologically it correlates with loss of the overlying epithelium plus surface necrotic debris, inflammation, and underlying granulation tissue, as seen in these venous, arterial, diabetic, and pressure-related chronic wounds.
explanation.whyCorrect: An ulcer is a wound lacking an intact epithelial surface; histologically it correlates with loss of the overlying epithelium plus surface necrotic debris, inflammation, and underlying granulation tissue, as seen in these venous, arterial, diabetic, and pressure-related chronic wounds.
